title: ScenarioTemplate
description: A human-authored scenario template (narrative + clue templates) at datasets/templates/<scenario>.json. Build-time authoring input. Mirrors tools/corpus.py ScenarioTemplate. The generator picks N values per category, samples a unique solution, emits clues from clueTemplates honoring the tier's indirection + compound gates, verifies uniqueness, then renders story + numbered clues.

evolution.change: Initial ScenarioTemplate v1: schemaVersion, id, title, subjectNoun, subjectCategory, ordinalAxis, minTier, maxTier, narrativeTemplate, flavorPools{ <slot> -> string[] }, indirection{byTier{tier->naming},note?}, categories[TemplateCategory], clueTemplates{ <type> -> ClueTemplate{minTier,variants[],appliesTo?,note?} }, note?.
evolution.why: Story-first pivot needs original, versioned narrative templates that drive verified-unique compound puzzles; typed before logic (CLAUDE.md sec 11).

evolution.change: Additive (backwards-compatible, schemaVersion stays 1): TemplateValue gains an optional `glyph` override - a value may carry its own '<pack>.<id>' ref, or '' to force text-only even under a category glyphPack. Old templates without it still validate.
evolution.why: The flowers pack backfilled only 2 of 6 blooms; a per-value override wires the covered blooms (jasmine/sunflower) without a broken ref on the uncovered ones, so a partly-backfilled pack can decorate the values it covers while the rest of the column stays text.
